problem: 4 12-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the number of dice showing a two digit number is equal to the number of dice showing a one digit number? Express your answer as a common fraction. (Assume that the numbers on the 12 sides are the numbers from 1 to 12 expressed in decimal.)

classified_questions.question: What is the problem asking us to find?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many total outcomes are there when rolling 4 12-sided dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we determine the probability of rolling a one-digit number on any one of the dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a two-digit number on any one of the dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the probability of rolling 2 two-digit numbers and 2 one-digit numbers among the 4 dice?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many different ways are there to choose which 2 dice will show one-digit numbers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we use the information from questions 5 and 6 to calculate the final probability that we are asked to find?
classified_questions.label: b
